description: A vertical 'Labor News' headline card from the U.S. Department of Labor. The photograph shows the exterior of the Frances Perkins Building (DOL headquarters): a tall dark monument-style sign in the right foreground reads 'Frances Perkins Building,' bears the gold Department of Labor seal, and the gold text 'United States Department of Labor' with a partial address '200 Constitution Ave NW' below; behind it is the building's tan concrete facade with a very large American flag hanging vertically across several stories at left. The lower portion fades to dark navy with a faint embossed DOL seal watermark at lower right. A small blue tab with white italic serif text reads 'Labor News' (with three vertical accent bars). Below, white serif headline text reads: 'US Department of Labor launches new tools to help businesses strengthen supply chains, stop unfair foreign labor practices.' Lower-right small white text reads 'DOL.GOV.' The style mimics a professional news graphic and uses patriotic institutional imagery (the flag and headquarters) for credibility. The messaging promotes the administration's 'America First' / 'American Workers First' agenda, framing new tools as protecting U.S. workers from unfair foreign competition. Target audience is the general public, businesses, and supply-chain stakeholders.
notable_text: Labor News - US Department of Labor launches new tools to help businesses strengthen supply chains, stop unfair foreign labor practices / DOL.GOV
